meta.title: Shopify vs Custom E-Commerce: Build vs Buy Comparison
meta.description: Structured comparison of Shopify (SaaS platform) versus a custom-built e-commerce solution across total cost, flexibility, time to market, and scalability.

shopify.notes: Days to weeks; theme selection, product import, payment setup, domain connect

custom.notes: 4 to 12 months for full-featured build; discovery, design, development, testing, launch

shopify.notes: Minimal; $0 to $300 for theme; Shopify plan from $39/mo; pay as you grow

custom.notes: $40,000 to $300,000+ for initial build depending on feature scope

shopify.notes: $39 to $399/mo + transaction fees; predictable; includes hosting, CDN, updates

custom.notes: $200 to $2,000/mo hosting + ongoing maintenance team or retainer

shopify.notes: Shopify Plus from $2,300/mo; transaction fees add up; 3rd-party app costs accumulate

custom.notes: Infrastructure cost grows slowly; no per-transaction fees; fixed engineering team cost

shopify.notes: Checkout Extensibility (Plus only) allows sections; core flow limited; no full control

custom.notes: 100% control; custom checkout steps, upsells, A/B testing, payment methods

shopify.notes: Liquid theme system powerful but constrained; Online Store 2.0 sections help; not pixel-perfect

custom.notes: Pixel-perfect design; any UI pattern possible; custom animations, interactions, layouts

shopify.notes: 8,000+ apps in App Store; most business needs covered; subscriptions, loyalty, reviews, ERP

custom.notes: Must build or integrate each feature from scratch; no plug-and-play apps

shopify.notes: Good baseline SEO; canonical URLs, sitemaps, meta control; limited URL structure control

custom.notes: Full URL control, custom structured data, server-side rendering, Core Web Vitals optimization

shopify.notes: Shopify handles Black Friday flash sales for Fortune 500 brands; infrastructure managed

custom.notes: Can scale to any load with proper architecture; requires engineering investment

shopify.notes: Native connectors for NetSuite, SAP, Brightpearl, ShipBob, dozens of 3PLs

custom.notes: Must build each integration; APIs exist but custom work required

shopify.notes: Vendor lock-in; data export available but migration is painful; dependent on Shopify policy changes

custom.notes: Full ownership of code, data, and infrastructure; portable to any hosting

shopify.notes: Shopify B2B (Plus only); company accounts, custom pricing, draft orders, net payment terms

custom.notes: Build any B2B workflow; custom quoting, approval chains, tiered pricing, PO integration

use_case_recommendations.reason: Launch in days, validate demand, zero upfront cost, app ecosystem covers growth needs

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Complex checkout with custom payment flows
use_case_recommendations.reason: Shopify checkout constraints block custom multi-step flows, split payments, or specialized installment logic

use_case_recommendations.use_case: High-GMV brand ($1M+/mo) seeking to reduce fees
use_case_recommendations.reason: Shopify Plus fees and 3rd-party app costs at scale often exceed custom build ROI threshold
Complex pricing logic, company hierarchies, and approval workflows exceed Shopify B2B capabilities
Shopify POS integrates natively; inventory sync across channels is production-ready out of box

use_case_recommendations.use_case: Embedded commerce in an existing SaaS product
use_case_recommendations.reason: Shopify cannot be embedded into another product's auth flow; custom checkout must own the UX

cost_breakeven_analysis.notes: Custom build typically breaks even vs Shopify Plus when GMV exceeds $500,000/mo and checkout fees plus app costs exceed $5,000/mo
